content.claim: Poland's 2017 reform reversal implementation caused acute disruption: closure of ~7,000 junior high schools, potential displacement of 10,000 teachers, and a double-cohort bottleneck in high-school admissions (e.g., 3,173 students unplaced in Warsaw alone).
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Disruption was specific to overlapping-cohort transition period; not necessarily endemic to all network restructuring
Does not isolate costs of reversal direction from costs of any rapid structural change
High-school capacity shortfall may reflect pre-existing under-investment rather than reform design alone
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
